description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a visually striking, textured appearance. The block is solid and appears to be made from a dark, speckled stone, possibly granite or basalt, with a slightly reflective, rough finish. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the heavily grooved top. The overall form is geometric and precise, suggesting it is manufactured or cut for a specific urban function. Its robust construction and weight indicate it is intended as a durable obstacle or barrier in public spaces.
description: A solid, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, made from dark, speckled stone. The block is heavy, durable, and visually textured, suitable as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface with the grooves appearing as horizontal lines.
description_view_1: Side-angled view emphasizing the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically in this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved pattern as horizontal lines across the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again highlighting the vertical grooves and their regular spacing.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced in a row with similar blocks
